zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Venables於1330年出生在英國英格蘭爾時，她的父親Hugh de Venables34歲，母親Katherine Houghton de Houghton22歲。她和Thomas Davenport of Henbury至少有2個兒子。她去世於1417年在她的家鄉享年87歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Venables于1330年出生在英国英格兰爾时，她的父亲Hugh de Venables34岁，母亲Katherine Houghton de Houghton22岁。她和Thomas Davenport of Henbury至少有2个儿子。她去世于1417年在她的家乡享年87岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Venables
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Venables (Eure), perhaps named with Late Latin venabulum ‘hunting ground’ (a derivative of venari ‘to hunt’).

variants.name: Venable
meanings: History: American bearers of this name are descended from Abraham Venables, who came to VA from England in or before 1687.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Venables was born in 1330, in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Hugh de Venables, was 34 and her mother, Katherine Houghton de Houghton, was 22. She had at least 2 sons with Thomas Davenport of Henbury. She died in 1417, in her hometown, at the age of 87.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret de Venables est née en 1330, à Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hugh de Venables, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Katherine Houghton de Houghton, avait 22 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec Thomas Davenport of Henbury. Elle est décédée en 1417, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 87 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret de Venables 1330 in Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh de Venables 34 und ihre Mutter Katherine Houghton de Houghton 22. Sie hatte mit Thomas Davenport of Henbury mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb 1417 im Alter von 87 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret de Venables è nata il nel 1330 nel Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Hugh de Venables, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Katherine Houghton de Houghton, aveva 22 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli con Thomas Davenport of Henbury. Morì nel 1417, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 87 anni.
